name: GT Alpina
classification_notes: Grilli call it a workhorse serif that plays with the meaning of the term, reaching into typographic history to resurrect shapes some may see as too expressive and pairing them with a pragmatic execution.
summary: A workhorse serif by Reto Moser that revives expressive historical shapes inside a pragmatic 70-style system.
description: A serif family by Reto Moser, released in 2020 and available in 70 styles. Grilli pitch it as a workhorse that delights in playing with the term, melding distinct historical shapes with a pragmatic execution. The family spans Condensed, Standard and Extended widths plus matching Fine cuts for display and a Typewriter set, with alternates for J, ?, & and @.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Alpina proudly calls itself a workhorse serif but delights in playing with the very meaning of that concept. They describe it as reaching into the grab bag of typographic history to resurrect shapes some may falsely see as too expressive, resulting in a meticulous family that melds those shapes with a pragmatic execution.

name: GT America
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as the missing bridge between 19th century American Gothics and 20th century European Neo-Grotesk typefaces, using the best design features from both traditions in the widths and weights where they function optimally.
summary: A 84-style neo-grotesque by Noël Leu that bridges American Gothics and European Neo-Grotesks across six widths.
description: A neo-grotesque sans by Noël Leu with Seb McLauchlan, released in 2016 and offered in 84 styles. Grilli position it as the missing bridge between 19th century American Gothics and 20th century European Neo-Grotesks, drawing the best of each tradition in the widths and weights where they work best. The family runs from Compressed through Expanded and includes a matching Mono, with Cyrillic, Greek and Vietnamese extensions.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type describe GT America as the missing bridge between 19th century American Gothics and 20th century European Neo-Grotesk typefaces. They say it uses the best design features from both traditions in the widths and weights where they function optimally, with script extensions and consulting from Tania Chacana, Maria Doreuli, Vassilis Georgiou and Donny Trương.

name: GT America Intl Collection
designer: Noël Leu with Grilli Type design team and expert partners
classification_notes: Grilli describe GT America as a bridge between American Gothics and European Neo-Grotesks. The Intl Collection adds Arabic, Devanagari, Hebrew, Korean, Thai and matching Simplified Chinese, Traditional Chinese and Japanese scripts to the Latin family.
summary: The multi-script extension of GT America, covering Arabic, Devanagari, Hebrew, Korean, Thai and CJK alongside Latin, Cyrillic and Greek.
description: The international collection built around Noël Leu's GT America, adding scripts designed by expert partners with the Grilli Type team. The Latin master ships in 84 styles and four variable fonts, with Cyrillic, Greek and Vietnamese as extensions and seven-weight Arabic, Devanagari, Hebrew, Korean and Thai cuts plus matching Simplified and Traditional Chinese (Arphic) and Japanese (Type Projects). It is the route into GT America for genuinely global identity systems.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type explain that the GT America Intl collection covers a wide range of writing systems and languages, with each script designed by expert partners together with Noël Leu and Grilli's design team. They note that the typeface remains the missing bridge between American Gothics and European Neo-Grotesks, with the Intl collection extending that bridge across scripts.

name: GT Canon
classification_notes: A large serif system built around five widths, three optical sizes (S, M, L) and a Mono companion, weighted from Light through Black with italics throughout.
summary: A large serif system from Grilli with five widths and three optical sizes plus a matching Mono.
description: A serif system from Grilli Type that pairs five widths (Condensed through Expanded) with S, M and L optical sizes across seven weights and italics, alongside a Mono companion. Sample texts on the page talk about glyph outlines, hinting and how scale introduces qualitative change, hinting at Canon's bookwork ambitions across small-text and display use.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type present GT Canon as a serif system built around three optical sizes (S, M, L) and five widths from Condensed to Expanded, with a matching Mono. They frame it through reflections on scale and precision, noting that even in fields predicated on precision, scale introduces qualitative change as a structural condition.

name: GT Cinetype
classification_notes: Based on a cinema subtitling machine that used a laser to erase the colour layer of the film. Because the laser only moved in straight lines, the typeface contains no curves.
summary: A curveless sans by Mauro Paolozzi and Rafael Koch that revives the laser-engraved subtitling alphabet of cinema.
description: A seven-style family by Mauro Paolozzi and Rafael Koch, released in 2015 and built on a design engineered for a cinema subtitling machine. Grilli explain that the laser used to erase the film's colour layer could only move in straight lines, so Cinetype contains no curves. Available across Light, Regular and Bold with italics and a Mono cut, it suits motion captions, cinema-leaning identities and any design that wants the texture of laser-cut text.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Cinetype is based on a design engineered for a cinema subtitling machine. They explain that by using a laser to erase the colour layer of the film, very small and brilliantly white letters appear, and that because the laser can only move in straight lines, the typeface contains no curves.

name: GT Eesti
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as a free-spirited interpretation of the Soviet geometric sans serif Zhurnalnaya Roublennaya, beginning from books printed in Soviet-occupied Estonia and developed into Display and Text subfamilies for Latin and Cyrillic.
summary: Reto Moser's free interpretation of the Soviet geometric sans Zhurnalnaya Roublennaya, split into Text and Display cuts for Latin and Cyrillic.
description: A geometric sans by Reto Moser, released in 2016 and offered in 28 styles. Grilli started from books printed in Soviet-occupied Estonia and developed Zhurnalnaya Roublennaya into separate Display and Text subfamilies for Latin and Cyrillic. The Text cut handles longer reading while the Display cut keeps the sharper geometric quirks for posters and headlines.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type describe GT Eesti as a free-spirited interpretation of the Soviet geometric sans serif Zhurnalnaya Roublennaya. They say they started with books from Soviet-occupied Estonia and developed the typeface to suit today's designers' requirements, expanding it into separate Display and Text subfamilies for both Latin and Cyrillic.

name: GT Era
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as a reimagining of the warmth and idiosyncrasies of early grotesk typefaces, the pre-modernist tools that were pushed to extremes by Bauhaus and De Stijl. The typeface shuns neutrality and embraces friction.
summary: Thierry Blancpain's reimagining of early grotesks in Display and Text optical sizes, choosing flavour over neutrality.
description: An early grotesk by Thierry Blancpain with Grilli Type, released in 2025 and offered in 28 styles across Display and Text. Grilli draw on pre-modernist grotesks of the period when Bauhaus and De Stijl pushed them to extremes, and say the family shuns neutrality and embraces friction. Seven weights from Thin to Black come with matching Obliques in each optical size.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Era reimagines the warmth and idiosyncrasies of early grotesk typefaces for our own era. They describe these pre-modernist tools as having been pushed to their extremes in the radical designs of Bauhaus and De Stijl, and say the typeface shuns neutrality and embraces friction, championing recognition over uniformity and flavour over conformity.

name: GT Flaire
classification_notes: Grilli say it captures the pronounced curves created by calligraphic pens when applying ink to paper under pressure, translating them into a digital format. It is built to bridge corporate and playful registers.
summary: Reto Moser's calligraphic sans translating pressured pen curves into a digital family across Basic and Extra cuts.
description: A calligraphic sans by Reto Moser, released in 2024 and offered in 28 styles split between Basic and Extra subfamilies. Grilli describe it as capturing the pronounced curves of a calligraphic pen pressed against paper, and as bridging the worlds of business and pleasure. Seven weights from Light to Extra come with italics, plus framed-letter alternates for added decorative range.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Flaire captures the pronounced curves created by calligraphic pens when applying ink to paper under pressure and translates this characteristic into a digital format. They describe it as bridging the worlds of business and pleasure, breaking free from corporate rigidity by combining seriousness with playfulness.

name: GT Flexa
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as a fluid design space rather than a collection of static styles. The 112-style system enables responsive typesetting across eight widths, including a dedicated Lazer cut and a Mono.
summary: A 112-style variable system by Dominik Huber and Marc Kappeler, built as a fluid design space for responsive typesetting.
description: A 112-style variable sans by Dominik Huber with Marc Kappeler, released in 2020. Grilli pitch it as a fluid design space rather than a static collection, allowing joyful, fully responsive typesetting. Eight widths from X Compressed to X Expanded plus a Mono each include a Lazer cut and italics, making it a strong fit for digital products and identity systems that need to flex with viewport.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say that instead of the traditional view of a typeface as a collection of static styles, GT Flexa embraces the idea of a fluid design space. They describe it as a dynamic tool that enables joyful typesetting and fully responsive designs, resulting in an extensive typographic system with a distinctive personality.

name: GT Haptik
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as a monolinear geometric grotesque whose uppercase letters and numbers were optimised to be read blindfolded and by touch. Available in seven weights with Oblique and Rotalic companions.
summary: Reto Moser and Tobias Rechsteiner's monolinear geometric grotesque, optimised to be read blindfolded and by touch.
description: A monolinear geometric grotesque by Reto Moser and Tobias Rechsteiner, released in 2010 and updated in 2014. Grilli explain that the uppercase letters and numbers were optimised to be read blindfolded and by touching them. The family now runs to 21 styles across seven weights, each with Oblique and Rotalic cuts plus alternate characters and proportional and tabular figures.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Haptik is a monolinear geometric grotesque typeface. They explain that its uppercase letters and numbers were optimised to be read blindfolded and by touching them, and that it is now available in seven weights with accompanying Oblique and Rotalic styles, with alternate characters and proportional and tabular figures.

name: GT Maru
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as an ode to rounded English characters on signage across Japan, exploring roundness (maru) in the Latin alphabet. Combines the warmth and flow of sign painting with the mechanical quality of engraved letters.
summary: Thierry Blancpain's rounded sans inspired by Japanese signage, with Mono, Mega and Emoji companions.
description: A rounded sans by Thierry Blancpain (with production by Huw Williams on Mega and Emoji work by Grilli Type with Anya Danilova), released in 2021 and offered in 25 styles. Grilli pitch it as an ode to rounded English characters found on Japanese signage, combining the warmth of sign painting with the mechanical quality of engraved letters. The system runs across GT Maru, Mono and Mega, with Mini, Midi and Maxi sizes plus colour and black-and-white Emoji.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Maru is an ode to rounded English characters found on signage across Japan. They describe the typeface as the result of a design exploration into roundness (maru) in the Latin alphabet, combining the warmth and flow of sign painting with the mechanical quality of engraved letters.

name: GT Mechanik
designer: Shiva Nallaperumal, Reto Moser, Noël Leu
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as dialling in the appeal of electromechanical text systems by building an inevitable family around its monospace style. Mono, Semi and Poly each carry that logic at different scales and intensities.
summary: A 42-style mono-derived system that runs from true Mono through Semi to fully proportional Poly.
description: A monospace-rooted system by Shiva Nallaperumal, Reto Moser and Noël Leu, released in 2026 and offered in 42 styles. Grilli describe the idiosyncratic features of electromechanical typesetting as guiding principles along a tone axis, with Mono, Semi and Poly each carrying that logic at different scales. Seven weights from Thin to Black come with Obliques in each cut, useful for editorial, technical and product UI work where you want a measured mechanical voice.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Mechanik dials in the appeal of electromechanical text systems by building an inevitable family around its monospace style. They explain that idiosyncratic features that come with the restraints of mechanic typesetting become guiding principles along the tone axis, with Mono, Text and Display each following that logic at a different scale and intensity.

name: GT Pantheon
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as an interpretation of historic shapes in a contemporary manner. Three optically-adjusted faces (Display, Text and Micro) represent the same type at different sizes, moving between elegance, sharpness, warmth and robustness.
summary: Tobias Rechsteiner and Noël Leu's three-optical-size serif, moving from Micro through Text to Display.
description: A 30-style serif by Tobias Rechsteiner and Noël Leu, released in 2024. Grilli describe Pantheon as an interpretation of historic shapes in a contemporary manner, with an expressive and dynamic character in every stroke. Three optically-adjusted faces (Micro, Text and Display) cover small captions through to headline sizes, moving between elegance, sharpness, warmth and robustness, with production by Antonio D'Elisiis.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Pantheon is an interpretation of historic shapes in a contemporary manner. They describe it as exhibiting an expressive and dynamic character visible in every stroke, with three optically-adjusted faces (Display, Text and Micro) designed to best represent the same type at different sizes, moving between elegance, sharpness, warmth and robustness.

name: GT Planar
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as testing the limits of a 90-degree range, combining Retalic, Upright and Italic styles into one continuous design space that transitions from -45 degrees Retalic to +45 degrees Italic.
summary: Dominik Huber's slant-axis sans that runs from -45 degree Retalic through Upright to +45 degree Italic.
description: A 42-style sans by Dominik Huber, released in 2022 and built on a continuous slant axis. Grilli describe it as testing the limits of a 90-degree range, transitioning seamlessly from -45 degree Retalic to +45 degree Italic while retaining its integrity. Six weights from Thin to Black sit at every 15-degree increment, with flat shape, curved number and single-story a and g alternates.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Planar tests the limits of a 90-degree range, combining Retalic, Upright and Italic styles into one continuous design space. They describe the typeface as transitioning seamlessly from -45 degree Retalic to +45 degree Italic while retaining its integrity, with functionality at any length, size or angle as its central star.

name: GT Pressura
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as inspired by metal type printing history and engineered letters stamped onto shipping boxes, using ink-spread under pressure as a stylistic device. Offered in Standard, Mono and Extended widths.
summary: Dominik Huber and Marc Kappeler's industrial sans modelled on ink-spread metal type and shipping-box stamps.
description: An industrial sans by Dominik Huber and Marc Kappeler (Moiré), released in 2012 and expanded in 2017 and 2022 to 36 styles. Grilli draw on metal-type ink spread and letters stamped on shipping boxes, using that visual gesture as a stylistic device. The family runs across Standard, Mono and Extended widths with italics throughout, plus Greek and Vietnamese extensions.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Pressura is inspired by metal type printing history as well as engineered letters stamped onto shipping boxes. They explain that it uses the visual gesture of ink spreading under pressure as a stylistic device, offering an alternative to more spindly typefaces of the digital age, with script extensions and consulting from Panos Haratzopoulos and Donny Trương.

name: GT Sectra
designer: Dominik Huber, Marc Kappeler (Moiré), Noël Leu
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as combining the calligraphy of the broad nib pen with the sharpness of the scalpel knife. Originally designed for the long-form magazine Reportagen and expanded into three subfamilies.
summary: A 30-style editorial serif combining broad-nib calligraphy with scalpel sharpness, originally drawn for Reportagen magazine.
description: A contemporary serif by Dominik Huber, Marc Kappeler (Moiré) and Noël Leu, released in 2013 and offered in 30 styles. Grilli pair broad-nib calligraphy with scalpel sharpness, and the family has expanded from its origins on Reportagen magazine into GT Sectra, Fine and Display subfamilies, with Cyrillic, Greek and Vietnamese script extensions consulted on by Maria Doreuli, Vassilis Georgiou and Donny Trương.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type describe GT Sectra as a contemporary serif typeface combining the calligraphy of the broad nib pen with the sharpness of the scalpel knife. They explain that it was originally designed for the long-form journalism magazine Reportagen and has since been expanded to its three subfamilies: GT Sectra, GT Sectra Fine and GT Sectra Display.

name: GT Standard
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as a contemporary response to the modernist pursuit of standardisation, rooted in Swiss Style and built for clarity, adaptability and precision across every scale and medium.
summary: Grilli's 336-style Swiss-style system, built as a contemporary response to modernist standardisation.
description: A 336-style Swiss sans by Grilli Type, released in 2025. Grilli pitch it as a contemporary response to the modernist pursuit of standardisation, rooted in Swiss Style and built for clarity, adaptability and precision. The family spans six widths from Compressed to Expanded plus a Mono family in three widths, each at S, M and L optical sizes with Obliques and seven weights, suited to large-scale design systems.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type describe GT Standard as a contemporary response to the modernist pursuit of standardisation. They say it is rooted in the principles of Swiss Style and expands on that legacy to meet the needs of today's visual landscape, with a systematic yet expressive typeface built for clarity, adaptability and precision across every scale and medium.

name: GT Super
designer: Noël Leu with Mirco Schiavone and Reto Moser
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as the result of an extensive investigation into 1970s and 80s display serifs, focused on the expressive and idiosyncratic nature of calligraphic motions held inside stable typographic shapes.
summary: Noël Leu's 1970s-80s display serif, available in Text and Display optical sizes across 20 styles.
description: A display serif by Noël Leu with Mirco Schiavone and Reto Moser, released in 2018 and offered in 20 styles. Grilli built it on an extensive investigation into 1970s and 80s display serifs, holding their expressive, calligraphic motions inside stable typographic shapes. Text and Display subfamilies cover both editorial and headline work, peaking in the swooning Display Super cut.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Super is the result of an extensive investigation into display serif typefaces from the 1970s and 80s. They describe it as focusing on the expressive and idiosyncratic nature of calligraphic motions, compelled into stable, typographic shapes.

name: GT Ultra
designer: Noël Leu with Huw Williams and Grilli Type
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as dancing between the worlds of sans and serif, fusing calligraphy and construction. The system challenges its own definition and questions contemporary typographic expectations.
summary: Noël Leu's 33-style semi-serif that dances between sans and serif worlds across Standard, Median and Fine cuts.
description: A 33-style semi-serif by Noël Leu with Huw Williams and Grilli Type, released in 2021. Grilli describe it as fusing calligraphy and construction across three subfamilies (Standard, Median and Fine) that move from soft to sharp. Six weights up to Ultra come with italics and a single-story g alternate, suited to identities that want serif gravitas with sans-leaning clarity.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Ultra dances between the worlds of sans and serifs, fusing calligraphy and construction. They describe the versatile typographic system as combining the centuries-old context of serif type with the dynamism of modern sans, challenging its own definition and questioning contemporary typographic expectation.

name: GT Walsheim
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as inspired by the lettering of Swiss poster designer Otto Baumberger from the 1930s, a friendly but precise typeface that supports all Cyrillic languages.
summary: Noël Leu's friendly geometric sans inspired by Otto Baumberger's 1930s Swiss poster lettering, in Standard and Condensed widths.
description: A geometric sans by Noël Leu, released in 2009 and updated in 2017 to 32 styles. Grilli draw it from Swiss poster designer Otto Baumberger's 1930s lettering, framing it as friendly but precise. Eight weights from Ultra Light to Ultra Bold come with Obliques in both Standard and Condensed widths, and the typeface supports all Cyrillic languages, which makes it a heavyweight identity workhorse.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type say GT Walsheim is inspired by the lettering of Swiss poster designer legend Otto Baumberger from the 1930s. They describe it as a friendly but precise typeface and note that it supports all Cyrillic languages.

name: GT Zirkon
classification_notes: Grilli describe it as an extravagant sans serif workhorse that blends rational tool and ornamentation by applying small-size optimisation techniques in a refined way.
summary: Tobias Rechsteiner's 16-style workhorse sans that applies small-size optimisation as a refined design feature.
description: A 16-style sans by Tobias Rechsteiner, released in 2018. Grilli call it an extravagant sans serif workhorse that blends rational tool and ornamentation by applying small-size optimisation techniques in a refined way. Eight weights from Ultra Light to Black come with italics throughout, plus alternates including an Alt f and old-style and tabular figures.
foundry_blurb: Grilli Type describe GT Zirkon as an extravagant sans serif workhorse. They say it blends the worlds of rational tool and ornamentation by applying techniques used to optimise type for small sizes in a refined way.
